data.title: Equinor delays start-up of Johan Castberg oilfield to early 2025
data.description:
Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) said Tuesday that the startup of its Johan Castberg offshore oilfield has been postponed to January or February from the end of 2024 due to bad weather conditions in the Barents Sea.

Johan Castberg holds estimated recoverable resources of 450M-650M boe and is expected to produce for 30 years; 14 wells have been drilled on the field so far, with 12 ready for production, which is sufficient to meet the plateau capacity of 220K bbl/day, the company said.

Equinor (EQNR) operates the field and owns a 50% interest, while Eni's (E) Vaar Energi and Norway's Petoro own respective 30% and 20% stakes.

Vaar said separately that the start-up delay would cause its FY 2024 production to come in at the lower end of its 280K-290K boe/day guidance range.

data.title: MoneyLion soars after news on proposed acquisition by Gen Digital
data.description:
Gen Digital (NASDAQ:GEN) is set to acquire the financial technology company MoneyLion (NYSE:ML) for a cash value of ~$1B.

GEN shares were trading +0.59% Tuesday pre-market at $30.66, while ML soared 10.38% to $85.00.

The boards of both the companies have unanimously approved the proposed acquisition for $82.00 per share in cash, payable at closing.

Additionally, MoneyLion shareholders will receive one contingent value right for each share at closing. The CVR entitles the holder to a contingent payment of $23.00 in the form of Gen shares.

Gen Digital also said that there can be no assurance that any payments will be made with respect to the CVRs.

The CVRs are expected to be listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market.

The transaction is expected to close in the first half of Gen's fiscal year 2026.

The acquisition is accretive to the company's non-GAAP EPS.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Retail sales rose in November despite Cyber Monday falling in December
data.description:
The CNBC/NRF Retail Monitor indicated that retail sales still grew in November even though two days of the Thanksgiving holiday fell in December.

Total retail sales, excluding automobiles and gasoline, were up 0.15% seasonally adjusted month over month and up 2.35% unadjusted year over year in November, according to the Retail Monitor. That compared with increases of 0.74% month over month and 4.13% year over year in October.

"November sales increased on top of a strong October and would have been even higher if Thanksgiving Sunday and Cyber Monday hadn't fallen in December," stated National Retail Federation CEO Matthew Shay said. "Year-over-year gains were solid even as retail prices in many categories are lower this year, showing that consumers are buying more merchandise as the economy continues to grow," he added. Notably, the NRF said it remains confident in its holiday forecast.

The online and other non-store sales category was strong in November, up 1.32% month over month seasonally adjusted and up 21.48% year over year unadjusted. Grocery and beverage stores were up 0.95% month over month seasonally adjusted and up 5.31% year over year unadjusted. Meanwhile, electronics and appliance stores were down 2.19% month over month seasonally adjusted and down 7.28% year over year unadjusted. The sporting goods, hobby, music and book stores category also disappointed, were down 1.2% month over month seasonally adjusted and down 7.39% year over year unadjusted.

Total sales were up 2.15% year over year for the first 11 months of 2024 and core sales were up 2.33%.

data.title: Rezolve AI gains as Roth MKM starts with Buy rating
data.description:
• Rezolve AI (NASDAQ:RZLV) shares gained 4% in premarket trading on Tuesday as research firm Roth MKM started coverage on the generative AI company with a Buy rating and $4 price target.
  • "RZLV's AI solutions transform the e-commerce sales process to mimic an in- store interaction with a trained salesperson," Roth MKM analyst Rohit Kulkarni wrote in a note to clients. "These AI products utilize agentic tech to simplify how a user searches, compares, interacts with customer service, and performs post-sale inquiries. Commerce platforms and retailers work with RZLV to include chatbots and conversational workflows in all aspects of marketing, commerce, customer service, and payments."
  • In addition, the recent deals with Microsoft (MSFT) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) "increase credibility and provide [near-term] conviction," Kulkarni added. "Our diligence calls with MSFT plus LLM benchmarking increase our confidence in RZLV's near-term trajectory."

data.title: Nano Labs buys bitcoins worth $5.5M
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) said it has invested in bitcoins (BTC-USD), and now owns ~55.6 BTC, valued at about $5.5 million as of December 10.
  • The firm purchased bitcoin at an average price of ~$99,400 per BTC.
  • This comes after the company has said earlier in December that it plans to purchase and hold $50 million worth of bitcoin (BTC-USD) over the next five years.
